what_it_does: This file implements the React project-creation wizard screen. It provides WizardScreen, a two-phase adaptive flow where an intake interview (UNDERSTAND) feeds into shape steps (Direction, Renderer, Sequence, Ready), plus a preserved alternate WizardScreenStream that delegates initialization to a StreamRunner. It also contains the reusable shape-phase components, a voice-capture overlay, helper UI pieces, and static DIRECTIONS / RENDERERS catalogs.
observations: Two wizard implementations coexist in one file: the restored WizardScreen and the newer WizardScreenStream.
The shape phase is driven by local React state (phase, shapeStep) and passes project/update/next/prev callbacks to each step.
AI calls to MS_SYNTH_AI fall back to static data when unavailable or when the call fails.
Shape-phase components are exported onto window so a separate StreamRunner scope can render them.
VoiceCapture uses the browser SpeechRecognition API and returns a transcript to a caller.
A Deductions side panel mirrors the evolving brief during the shape phase.
Static DIRECTIONS and RENDERERS arrays define preset creative directions and rendering modes.
MoodTile synthesizes a CSS gradient placeholder when a direction has no real anchor image.
standouts: The file deliberately preserves two competing initialization flows side by side with comments explaining the convergence intent.
It relies heavily on global window objects (React hooks aliased with Z, MS_INTAKE, MS_CONTEXT, MS_SYNTH_AI, ViContext).
AI-generated directions and sequences have explicit static fallback paths.
conventions: React functional components with hooks renamed with a Z suffix (useStateZ, useEffectZ, etc.).
Global registration via window.WizardScreen and Object.assign(window, ...).
Static fallback catalogs used when AI services are unavailable.
Inline style objects mixed with CSS class names.
Props pattern of project/update/next/prev passed through shape steps.
concerns: Two wizard implementations in one file risk divergence and maintenance burden.
Heavy dependency on window globals makes the module hard to test or reason about in isolation.
shapeNext and shapePrev are redefined on every render inside WizardScreen.
StepSequence uses array index as React key.
StepReady mixes React.useEffect directly while other hooks use the aliased Z versions.
Several consumed components (Icon, Btn, Field, Chip, ViMark, IntakeInterview, StreamRunner) are not defined or imported in this file.
Regeneration uses Math.random as a nonce.
VoiceCapture speech recognition cleanup is minimal.
notes: This is the wizard screen file that keeps both the original two-phase adaptive setup and a newer StreamRunner-based variant, exposing reusable shape components and static fallback catalogs.
questions: Which of the two wizard components is actually mounted by the application router?
Where are MS_INTAKE, MS_SYNTH_AI, MS_CONTEXT, ViContext, Icon, Btn, Field, Chip, ViMark, IntakeInterview, and StreamRunner defined or imported?
Why is the initialPrompt threshold set to six characters?
How does the StreamRunner init stream differ functionally from the hand-coded shape steps?
What is the tweaks / setTweak contract used by WizardScreenStream?
How is the project brief persisted after onComplete is called?
What are the eight anti-pattern memory rules referenced in Deductions?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the comments state the file restores the original two-phase adaptive wizard while preserving the newer StreamRunner-based variant so both can coexist during convergence, and the code literally contains both flows.
apparent_themes: Human-in-the-loop creative direction and renderer selection
Graceful degradation when AI is unavailable
Coexistence of legacy and stream-driven architectures
Vi as an embodied design assistant
intention_signals: The file is trying to give users a guided, conversational project setup where Vi interviews them, synthesizes a creative direction and renderer, drafts a state sequence, and then creates the project; simultaneously it tries to keep an older hand-coded wizard and a newer stream-driven runner both available while they are being merged.
novelty: Distinctive aspects include the deliberate side-by-side preservation of two initialization architectures in one module, and the export of local shape-phase components to a separate StreamRunner Babel scope so the same UI can be reused across flows.
contribution: A concrete UI implementation of a two-phase project wizard with reusable shape-step components, static fallback catalogs for directions/renderers/sequences, a voice-capture overlay, and a preserved StreamRunner-based variant that exposes the shape components for stream reuse.
summary_for_embedding: A React wizard screen that implements an adaptive two-phase project setup (intake interview followed by direction, renderer, sequence, and ready steps) alongside a preserved StreamRunner-based variant, with reusable shape components and static fallback data.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a conversational, AI-assisted project onboarding flow that interviews the user, synthesizes creative choices, drafts a state sequence, and creates the project while keeping an older wizard and a newer stream runner both reachable.

symbols.does: Advances the shape phase step index toward the final step.
symbols.description: A callback created inside WizardScreen that increments shapeStep, capping it at the last index of SHAPE_STEPS.

symbols.does: Moves the shape phase step index backward, or returns to the understand phase from the first shape step.
symbols.description: A callback created inside WizardScreen that decrements shapeStep, or switches phase back to understand when already at step zero.

symbols.does: Renders a voice-input overlay that captures speech and returns the final transcript.
symbols.description: A component that starts the browser SpeechRecognition API, accumulates final and interim transcripts, and calls onResult with the transcript when the user confirms.

symbols.does: Renders a styled chat bubble with an avatar for Vi messages.
symbols.description: A presentational component that displays Vi-branded avatar rings and a text body, optionally with an action slot.

symbols.does: Renders an animated thinking indicator for Vi.
symbols.description: A presentational component showing Vi avatar rings, animated dots, and a label while waiting for AI work.

symbols.does: Generates a CSS gradient placeholder tile for a direction with no anchor image.
symbols.description: A component that builds a warm radial and linear gradient background, adds decorative rectangles, an accent line, and mood tags based on a palette prop.

symbols.does: Lets the user pick a creative direction, optionally generated by AI or falling back to static options.
symbols.description: The first shape step. It fetches AI-generated directions via MS_SYNTH_AI, displays them as selectable cards with thumbnails or MoodTile placeholders, supports regeneration, and records the chosen direction and renderer.

symbols.does: Requests fresh AI-generated directions for the project.
symbols.description: An async callback inside StepDirection that calls MS_SYNTH_AI.directions with a random nonce and updates the project with the new result.

symbols.does: Returns a static fallback list of directions when AI is unavailable.
symbols.description: Maps the DIRECTIONS catalog into the shape expected by StepDirection, filling in default palette and rationale values and marking one direction as recommended.

symbols.does: Lets the user choose a renderer for the selected direction.
symbols.description: The second shape step. It highlights the renderer suggested by the chosen direction and lets the user pick any renderer from the RENDERERS catalog.

symbols.does: Displays and edits an AI-drafted or fallback state sequence.
symbols.description: The third shape step. It fetches or falls back to a sequence of states, renders them as an editable timeline, allows renaming, removing, and adding states, and shows a generation cost estimate.

symbols.does: Appends a blank state to the sequence draft.
symbols.description: A callback inside StepSequence that adds a new state with placeholder name and sub text.

symbols.does: Removes a state from the sequence draft by index.
symbols.description: A callback inside StepSequence that filters the states array to exclude the given index.

symbols.does: Updates the name of a state in the sequence draft.
symbols.description: A callback inside StepSequence that maps over states and replaces the name of the state at the given index.

symbols.does: Presents a final summary and lets the user name and create the project.
symbols.description: The fourth shape step. It shows the project title, goal, direction, renderer, sequence, and material, auto-suggests a title, and calls onCreate with the final brief.

symbols.does: Renders a side panel summarizing what Vi has learned so far.
symbols.description: A component that displays the current goal, direction, renderer, sequence draft, auto-attach list, and a voice-call-to-action during the shape phase.

symbols.does: Truncates a string to a maximum length with an ellipsis.
symbols.description: A small utility function that returns the original string if it is within the limit, otherwise slices it and appends an ellipsis.

symbols.does: Returns a default state sequence based on direction and renderer.
symbols.description: A fallback function that chooses a template sequence from several hard-coded spines depending on the selected direction or renderer.

symbols.does: Suggests a project title from the brief.
symbols.description: A utility that derives a title from the project concept, falling back to a combination of the selected direction title and renderer label.
